calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Continued To Jul-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator Jaime Barros to appear on Jul-06-2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. The court orders Administrator to pay sanctions in the amount of $300. The court orders the Administrator suspended and an Order to Show Cause as to why he should not be removed as Administrator and as to why the court should not order an additional $300 in sanctions is set for Jul-06-2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Petrucci. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Continued To Jul-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called for hearing for status regarding close of estate and for order to show cause. No appearances were made by any party or counsel.

The Court imposed sanctions in the amount of $300 against administrator Jaime Barros for failure to appear. The Court further suspended Mr. Barros as administrator of the estate.

The Court continued the matter to May 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 and stated that an Order to Show Cause re Removal of Administrator shall issue.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Administrator in pro per Jaime Barros for Mar-16-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204 for failure to appear and to show cause why sanctions in the amount of $300.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear and why he should not be Suspend or Removed as Administrator. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Continued To Feb-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Jaime Barros (administrator) to appear on February 9, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge:Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cece. Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel, moving party seeking to be relieved as counsel to administrator Jaime Barros.

The Court noted that administrator Jaime Barros did not appear, and inquired whether Mr. Wrobel had received any objection or opposition to his motion to be relieved as counsel. Mr. Wrobel stated that he had not heard from his client and that there had been a complete breakdown in communication.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

Judge: Lydia Villarreal; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
